segments.text:
nuclear envelope
Double lipid bilayer membrane: inner and outer
Inner: interacts with chromatin and lamina
Outer: continuous with ER
Nuclear pores

NUCLEAR PORE COMPLEX
Large complex of dozens of proteins (nucleoporins)
Regulates movement between cytosol and nucleus
Components: structural ring, filaments, nuclear basket

GATED DIFFUSION BARRIER
Large unstructured domains block diffusion
FG-repeats in unstructured domains
Cutoff size = ~ 40 kD

HOW ARE NUCLEAR PROTEINS MARKED FOR IMPORT?
Nuclear proteins synthesized in cytosol
Nuclear localization signal (NLS)
first discovered in SV40 T-antigen
different NLS sequences exist

NUCLEAR LOCALIZATION SIGNAL
Necessary and sufficient for nuclear import
If added to cytosolic protein, then protein imported into nucleus (can be added anywhere in sequence)
Normal Cytosolic Protein
Cytosolic Protein with NLS added

IMPORTINS
Many different types – each binds specific NLS
Help diffusion of cargo through NPC
Bind to NLS and FG-repeats
FG-repeats: common in filaments and unstructured domains of NPC proteins

DIRECTIONALITY REGULATION FACTORS
Ran
small GTP binding protein
required for both import and export
2 forms: GTP bound and GDP bound
Ran-GAP: GTPase-activating protein
Ran-GEF: guanine nucleotide-exchange factor

RAN REGULATION
Ran-GAP: localized to cytosol
Ran-GEF: localized to nucleus
Cytosolic Ran: GDP bound
Nuclear Ran: GTP bound

NUCLEAR IMPORT OF PROTEINS
Ran-GTP triggers release of cargo

NUCLEAR EXPORT OF PROTEINS
Nuclear export signal (NES):  often leucine-rich sequence
Exportins bind to NES and FG-repeats
Ran-GTP promotes cargo binding

NUCLEAR EXPORT OF mRNA
NXF1 (nuclear export factor 1) and NXT1 (nuclear export transporter 1) bind mRNA and FG-repeats
Dbp5 (helicase) removes NXF1/NXT1 on cytosolic side

CYTOSOLIC-NUCLEAR SHUTTLING PROTEINS
NF-AT example
transcription factors involved in immune response
NLS masked when NF-AT phosphorylated
activated calcineurin unmasks NLS and masks NES
